name: Wild Rice-Stuffed Winter Squash
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Mushrooms (wild variety such as crimini, shiitake, morel or oyster) - sliced
1/2 cup Hazelnuts or walnuts - lightly toasted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the squash in half lengthwise and place cut side down in a lightly greased baking dish. Bake at 350°F until just tender. Scoop out seeds and set aside.
2. Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms, garlic, and dill seed and sauté for 1 to 2 minutes, until the mushrooms appear moist.
3. Stir in green onions, wild rice, 1/3 cup nuts, and parsley. Season with pepper and salt to taste.
4. Spoon the stuffing mixture into squash halves and press with the back of a spoon to fill the cavity completely. Arrange in a lightly greased baking dish. Sprinkle reserved nuts on top.
5. Cover and bake at 350°F for about 30 minutes, until the squash is steaming when you lift the lid. Remove the cover and bake an additional 10 minutes, until the top of the filling is crisp. Serves 4.
Helpful hint: Hazelnuts have a better flavor if skins are removed. Bake shelled nuts in a preheated 325°F oven for about 10 minutes, then wrap them in a kitchen towel and rub off as much of the skins as possible.
